assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can run Node.js tooling locally.
No Discord user account, Developer Portal application, bot token, or interaction endpoint exist yet.
The developer can create or use a test server where they hold MANAGE_GUILD for installation.

developer_goal: Create a Discord developer app and complete the official first-bot tutorial through an interactive success the guide treats as finished.

first_success.outcome: A configured and installed Discord bot receives interactions through a public endpoint and completes the tutorial's rock-paper-scissors challenge flow.
first_success.observable_signal: The finished two-user /challenge game posts the calculated result to the original channel.

route_selection.surface: Discord developer docs Bots & Companion Apps getting-started tutorial
route_selection.rule: Prefer the documented first-bot tutorial over Activities and Social SDK when measuring a common Discord app path.
route_selection.selected: Building your first Discord Bot (JavaScript example app)

candidates.name: Building your first Discord Bot
candidates.reason: Overview's Start Building section leads with Bots & Companion Apps, and the linked Getting Started guide is the end-to-end first-bot tutorial; it redirects iframe game builders to Activities.

candidates.name: Building your first Activity
candidates.reason: It is a separate Embedded App SDK outcome; the bot guide explicitly points Activity builders elsewhere.

candidates.name: Discord Social SDK for games
candidates.reason: It targets in-game social features rather than a server bot first success.

required_path.action: Create a Discord user account if one does not already exist.

required_fields.label: Discord registration fields
required_fields.notes: The bot guide assumes an available Discord account; the public register page is a JavaScript app whose fields were not readable from static HTML.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can sign in to the Discord Developer Portal.

required_path.action: Clone discord/discord-example-app and install npm dependencies.
required_path.observable_result: The local example project is ready for credentials.

required_path.action: Create a Discord application in the Developer Portal and enter an app name.
required_path.observable_result: The app General Information page shows Application ID and related settings.

required_path.action: Copy Application ID, Public Key, and a reset Bot token into the project's .env file.
required_path.observable_result: Local environment credentials required by the sample app are present.

required_path.action: Configure Installation contexts, Discord Provided Link, default scopes, and bot Send Messages permission.

required_fields.label: User Install and Guild Install contexts

required_path.observable_result: An install link is ready for server and user installation.

required_path.action: Install the app to a test server and to the user account via the install link prompts.
required_path.observable_result: The app appears in the test server member list and as a user-installed app.

required_path.action: Register slash commands, start the local server, expose it with ngrok, and save the Interactions Endpoint URL.
required_path.observable_result: Discord verifies the interaction endpoint and slash commands are available.

required_path.action: Add the documented /challenge and component handlers required to finish the tutorial game.
required_path.observable_result: The app contains the complete challenge interaction path.

required_path.action: Run the two-user /challenge flow through Accept and object selection.
required_path.observable_result: The calculated game result is posted to the original channel.

prerequisites.description: Node.js, Git, npm, a code editor, and a terminal.
A test server where the developer can authorize installation with MANAGE_GUILD, or permission to create a free server.
ngrok or another public HTTPS tunnel for the local interactions endpoint.

external_gates.description: Server installation requires a member with MANAGE_GUILD to authorize the bot.
Discord must be able to reach a publicly accessible Interactions Endpoint URL.

unavoidable_waits.description: Global slash-command propagation may lag after registration before commands appear in the client.

platform_outcomes.description: Discord creates the application and bot user, and issues credentials used by the sample app.
Discord verifies the interactions endpoint and delivers component and command interactions to the running app.

excluded.item: Building an Activity instead of a bot
excluded.reason: The selected guide redirects iframe/game builders to the Activity tutorial.
The getting-started path ends at local tutorial success in a test server.

excluded.item: Reading overview documentation as a counted action
excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence, not a product interaction.

sources.title: Overview of Discord Apps
evidence_supported: Bots, Activities, and Social SDK app types
Start-building links for bots versus Activities

sources.title: Building your first Discord Bot
evidence_supported: Installation contexts, scopes, and install link

uncertainties.question: What exact Discord account registration steps precede Developer Portal access for a new person?
uncertainties.impact: Account-creation field counts at the audit boundary remain unverified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated walkthrough of discord.com/register through first Developer Portal login.

uncertainties.question: Should first success stop at /test after endpoint verification, or continue through the full two-user /challenge game the tutorial presents as the finished app?
uncertainties.impact: Choosing the earlier /test checkpoint would shorten required implementation actions versus the guide's end-state narrative.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus decision on whether tutorial midpoints count as first success when the guide's stated finished app is the longer game flow.

uncertainties.question: How long do global slash commands currently take to appear after registration?
uncertainties.impact: Unavoidable wait duration and whether developers must retry remain incompletely specified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Current official guidance or a fresh-account observation of command propagation after npm run register.
